sections.text:
3. (1) The President may enter into an agreement, with any
One-stop
border neigbouring State or territory with a view to facilitating trade.
agreements
The President may delegate the powers conferred under
subsection (1) to the Minister.
An agreement entered into pursuant to subsection (1) shall
allow the following:
(a) border controls to be undertaken by officers at a place
located-
in the territory of Zambia; or
within the territory of one or more adjoining States;
and
(b) officers of the Zambian Government, and officers of one
or more adjoining States, to exercise border controls in
accordance with the relevant legislation, including the
exercise of any powers of arrest, search, seizure and
detention of persons and goods.
The Minister shall lay a copy of an agreement referred to
in sebsection (1) before Cabinet approval prior to signing of the
agreement.
The Minister shall, within fourteen days of the signing of
agreement, publish the agreement in the Gazette.
One-Stop Border Control [ No. 8 of 2009 49
The relevant legistation of Zambia and that of an adjoining Application
State shall apply in the control zone for the purpose of enabling of relevant
legislation in
officers of Zambia, and those of any adjoining State which is a
control zone
party to an agreement, to carry out border controls at a one-stop
border post.
(1) An act or omission— Application
of criminal
by a citizen or resident of Zambia or a person having
law
entered Zambia which occurs at a one-stop border
post in an adjoining State; and
that is an act or ommission which, if it had occured
within the territory of Zambia, would have
constituted an offence under the laws of Zambia;
shall be treated as if it had occured in Zambia.
For the avoidance of doubt, a citizen or resident of Zambia
or a person having entered Zambia may be arrested or detained in
the control zone of an adjoining State for any contravention of the
relevant legislation of Zambia, for the purpose of transferring that
person to Zambia.
For the purpose of determining which court has jurisdiction
to try an offence referred to in subsection (1), the offence shall be
deemed to have been committed within the area of the court with
competent jurisdiction nearest to the place where the act or omission
constituting the offence occured.
(1) The legislation listed in the Schedule to this Act relates to Legislation
a one-stop border post and shall be the basis for border control related to
one-stop
operations by the institution specified in the legislation:
border post
Provided that where the provisions of the relevant legislation
are in conflict with this Act, the provisions of this Act shall apply.
(2) The Minister may, by regulation, add or remove any legislation
from the Schedule.
The Minister may, by statutory instrument, make regulations Regulation
providing for any matter which by this Act is required to be
prescribed or which is necessary to be prescribed to give effect to
the purpose of this Act.

sections.heading: An agreement entered into before the commencement of Transitional
sections.text:
8. An agreement entered into before the commencement of Transitional
this Act shall be deemed to have complied with the provisions of provision
this Act and shall be implemented in accordance with the provisions
of this Act.
